riskReasons: Java 8 to Java 25 crosses multiple LTS baselines

workItems.title: Run baseline tests in CI before migration changes
workItems.rationale: A Java migration needs a known failing/passing baseline before changing runtime, framework, or build configuration.

workItems.title: Run OpenRewrite Java 25 recipe in a branch
workItems.rationale: OpenRewrite automation should be reviewable and separate from manual runtime changes.

findings.title: Java 8 baseline should be migrated deliberately before adopting Java 25
findings.recommendation: Establish a Java 25 build and test baseline before introducing optional language modernization.

findings.title: Spring Boot 2.x needs compatibility validation before a Java 25 migration
findings.recommendation: Validate the project on Spring Boot 2.7.x before the Java 25 rollout. Treat Spring Boot 3 as a separate migration because it also introduces Jakarta namespace changes.

findings.title: maven-surefire-plugin should be upgraded before Java 25 test baselining
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Surefire/Failsafe to a Java 25 compatible baseline before trusting migration test results.

findings.title: Maven compiler configuration should be explicit for Java 25 migration evidence
findings.evidence: No maven-compiler-plugin entry was detected in build plugins
findings.recommendation: Add explicit compiler plugin configuration or verify the effective POM so the report can explain the Java release used by CI.

findings.title: OpenRewrite has a Java 25 migration recipe
findings.recommendation: Review and run the suggested OpenRewrite recipe in a branch before manual changes.

findings.title: Map-based response can be reviewed as an explicit DTO or record
findings.recommendation: Review whether this loosely typed map represents a stable response shape. If it does, model it as a DTO now and consider a record when the target runtime supports it.

findings.title: Reflective access should be reviewed before Java 11+ migration
findings.recommendation: Run tests on Java 11 with warnings enabled and remove or explicitly contain illegal reflective access.

findings.title: Removed Java EE/JAXB API usage blocks a clean Java 11 migration
findings.recommendation: Add explicit dependencies or migrate the affected API before moving the runtime to Java 11.
